what_it_does: This file is a generated surface specification for view A2: a JSON manifest that records what the view represents, which UI addresses it consumes, which components it builds to, how it is linted, and which token file drives its styling.
observations: The file is a generated surface-spec for view id 'A2' in area 'A' titled 'Canvas — running chain (kind-coded)'.
Status is 'redo-pre-audit', indicating it is not yet approved.
home_journey is '_unrouted' while 'journeys' and 'cross_journeys' are empty arrays.
'represents' lists conceptual concerns: NODE-kinds, RUN-status, ENG-resolve, NODE-ports, CAN-zoom.
'addresses' contains 13 ui:// scheme references to activity, board, canvas, chat, rail, toolbar, and workshop surfaces.
'build_to' names seven components, a design_lint command, and a tokens-to-CSS mapping.
The '_what' field documents that the file was built by a Claude Code session and warns never to invent an address.
standouts: Empty journeys/cross_journeys despite a populated address list.
Build instructions embed a concrete lint command and a token conversion path.
Use of a ui:// address scheme for stable UI references.
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, and build_to.
Uses short tag strings in 'represents' to signal conceptual coverage.
Lists target components, lint gate, and token source under 'build_to'.
Includes a self-describing '_what' preamble explaining provenance and constraints.
Breaks completeness by leaving routing arrays empty while addresses are present.
concerns: Status 'redo-pre-audit' suggests the spec needs rework before it can be trusted.
Empty 'journeys' and 'cross_journeys' may mean routing logic is missing or not yet captured.
The lint command path is hardcoded and could drift relative to the actual source tree.
No generated timestamp, version, or explicit dependency list.
No symbols are listed even though the file represents code/design substrates.
notes: A pre-audit, un-routed surface manifest for a canvas view that maps UI addresses to components and pins build-time lint and token sources.
questions: What audit criteria must pass to move out of 'redo-pre-audit'?
Why are 'journeys' and 'cross_journeys' empty when addresses are already defined?
How are the ui:// addresses validated against real components?
What does 'B3' refer to as the web carrier?
How is 'design/_system/tokens.json → design-system.css' actually generated and kept in sync?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a surface spec linking a view to addresses, components, lint, and tokens; the file does that, but the empty routing arrays and pre-audit status mean it is not yet a fully wired specification.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a canonical, machine-readable contract for a UI surface so that design intent, implementation targets, quality gates, and theming can be traced and built automatically.
novelty: The use of a ui:// address scheme as stable references for UI surfaces, plus embedding both a lint command and a token-to-CSS conversion directly in the surface spec.
contribution: This file contributes a structured surface manifest that records which UI addresses a view consumes, which components should implement them, how to lint the result, and which token source governs appearance; a fused system could keep this manifest pattern for build orchestration and design-system alignment.
summary_for_embedding: Generated JSON surface specification for view A2 ('Canvas — running chain (kind-coded)') listing UI address references, target components, a design-lint command, and a token-to-CSS source, with empty journey routing and a redo-pre-audit status.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as a build-ready design-to-code substrate that maps UI addresses to canvas-related components and enforces design lint and token sourcing.
